object.description: Ottoman embroidered panel, 18th century. Silk thread on linen ground, couched metallic supplement. Floral medallion composition with cartouche border. Approximately 64 x 89 cm.
object.condition_at_intake: Moderate. Warp breakage in upper left quadrant, approximately 12 percent of ground fabric. Localized tenting and cupping of embroidery silk in central medallion. Iron gall discoloration in lower right corner. Dust surface soiling throughout. Metallic threads tarnished but intact.

fiber_analysis.test: Ground woven in tabby linen; z-spun, approximately 28 threads per centimeter warp, 22 weft. Embroidery threads: silk, S-twist, 2-ply. Supplementary metallic: flat metal strip wound on fiber core, consistent with silver-gilt.

Ground fabric fluoresces cream-white, consistent with aged linen. Embroidery silk shows varied fluorescence; rose and gold areas quench under UV, indicating dye fugitivity risk during any aqueous treatment.

dye_analysis.note: Visual-spectroscopic spot testing only. No aqueous or solvent dye extraction performed due to metallic thread proximity. Red embroidery consistent with cochineal or lac; further analysis deferred to post-treatment sampling if client authorizes.

phase.title: Surface Cleaning
phase.step: Dry surface cleaning with soft natural-bristle brush under low suction (5 cm H2O), screen barrier in place. Dust removed from ground fabric and interstices.
Lint picking of loose fiber debris with bamboo point under 10x magnification.
Photography before and after: macro series at 1:1, raking light both directions, transmitted light.

phase.title: Humidification and Relaxation
phase.step: Object placed face-up in humidification chamber. Local ultrasonic humidifier, no direct wetting. Chamber RH raised to 68 percent over 4 hours.
Monitored tenting response in central medallion every 30 minutes. After 6 hours at 68 percent RH, tenting reduced from approximately 4 mm to 1 mm maximum relief.
Object removed from chamber and allowed to relax under light weight on Hollytex-interleaved board for 48 hours at controlled environment (50 percent RH, 20 degrees C).
Post-relaxation photography. Tenting in central medallion resolved to approximately 0.5 mm.

phase.title: Structural Stabilization - Warp Repair
phase.step: Warp breakage area isolated under 20x stereomicroscope. Broken warp ends counted: 47 threads across 5.8 cm horizontal span, upper left quadrant.
Prepared fine linen thread (26/2 nm, undyed) for replacement warp supports. Threads pre-aged in 1 percent sodium bicarbonate bath and rinsed, dried flat.
Consolidated adjacent weft with 3 percent Paraloid B-72 in ethanol, applied with micro-dropper, allowed to cure 24 hours before thread insertion.
Stitching consolidation of replacement warp supports in progress. Target completion 2026-02-28.

materials.material: Paraloid B-72, 3 percent solution in ethanol

phase.title: Mounting Preparation
phase.note: Mounting to follow full structural treatment. Exhibition mount format TBD with Elena Vasquez and MFA installation team. Linen-covered Dibond substrate proposed. Stitch-based attachment, no adhesive to object.
